label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see what the question is asking. The question is about why small pieces of space junk can threaten satellites. The options are A to D. I need to find the correct answer based on the provided passage. First, I'll read through the passage again to find the relevant part. The passage mentions that even bits of space junk as small as dots of paint pose a threat. Dana explains that this is because the debris orbits our planet at speeds up to 28,200 kilometers per hour. The high speed is why tiny paint dots have damaged space shuttle windshields so badly they needed replacement. So the key point here is the high speed of the debris. The answer should relate to their speed. Let's look at the options: A) They travel at a very high speed. That matches what the passage says. B) They are difficult to track by satellites. The passage doesn't mention tracking difficulty as the reason for the threat, but rather the speed causing damage. C) They can blow up into millions of pieces. The passage talks about collecting space junk but doesn't say that small pieces can explode into more pieces. D) They are as soft as a ball in the air.
